name: Jordan Rules
description: A famous, highly aggressive defensive strategy implemented by the Detroit Pistons in the late 1980s specifically to stop Michael Jordan. The rules involved forcing him to his weaker side (left), instantly double- or triple-teaming him the moment he touched the ball, and applying extreme physical contact whenever he drove to the basket to wear him down physically and mentally. It highlights a fundamental principle: adapting a team's entire defensive geometry and physicality to neutralize a single transcendent offensive talent.
